articles.title: Chuck Norris, martial arts master and actor whose toughness became internet lore, dies at 86
articles.description:
Before he would become a star in movies and on TV, Norris was wildly successful in competitive martial arts.

The post Chuck Norris, martial arts master and actor whose toughness became internet lore, dies at 86 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Chuck Norris, martial arts icon, passed away at age 86 on March 20, 2026.
Norris was a pioneer in competitive martial arts, influencing local martial arts schools in Boston.
His death may inspire tributes and events celebrating martial arts culture in the Boston area.

articles.title: Nine Winters Bakery is closing in Cambridge: ‘I’m choosing my family’
articles.description:
In an Instagram post, owner Marissa Ferola addressed the closure and said the last day of service is at the end of the month.

The post Nine Winters Bakery is closing in Cambridge: ‘I’m choosing my family’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Nine Winters Bakery in Cambridge will close on March 31, 2023.
Owner Marissa Ferola cited family priorities as the reason for the closure.
Local residents will lose a popular bakery option in the area.

articles.title: Stephen Curry swung by this North End restaurant while in town for Celtics-Warriors
articles.description:
"Where championship energy meets Italian indulgence."

The post Stephen Curry swung by this North End restaurant while in town for Celtics-Warriors appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Stephen Curry visited Strega in the North End on March 20, 2026.
Curry's presence attracted fans and boosted local restaurant visibility.
The visit highlights Boston's vibrant dining scene during major sporting events.

articles.title: It’s finally payday for former Boston Sports Clubs members
articles.description:
The Massachusetts Attorney General’s Office is distributing $4 million to members who the sports club improperly charged during the COVID-era shutdowns.

The post It’s finally payday for former Boston Sports Clubs members appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts AG distributes $4 million to former Boston Sports Clubs members.
Members improperly charged during COVID-era shutdowns will receive compensation.
Payments aim to rectify financial losses for local residents affected by the closures.

articles.title: Beat out by a hot tamale: Maine hosts the second-best specialty food festival in America, according to USA Today readers
articles.description:
The food festival takes place on June 13.

The post Beat out by a hot tamale: Maine hosts the second-best specialty food festival in America, according to USA Today readers appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Maine's specialty food festival ranked second in America by USA Today readers.
The festival is scheduled for June 13, attracting food enthusiasts from Boston.
Boston residents may travel to Maine for unique culinary experiences and local flavors.

articles.title: Man accused of recording people in South Shore library bathroom
articles.description:
Police say the hidden device was discovered by staff at the public library in Cohasset.

The post Man accused of recording people in South Shore library bathroom appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Police arrested a man for allegedly recording individuals in a Cohasset library bathroom.
The hidden recording device was discovered by library staff on March 20, 2026.
Residents express concern over privacy and safety in public spaces following the incident.

articles.title: Energy assistance runs out amid spiking oil prices
articles.description:
After a long, frigid winter and a sudden spike in heating oil prices, thousands across the state are scrambling to refill their tanks as the cold weather lingers.

The post Energy assistance runs out amid spiking oil prices appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Thousands of Boston residents face heating oil shortages amid rising prices.
Energy assistance programs have run out, leaving many without support.
Lingering cold weather exacerbates the crisis, increasing urgency for residents.
